rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KIM SENG TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The successor conservator of the estate was appointed on 5/4/23. Why does the I&A show assets appraised as of 5/8/23? (2) Schedule F did not show the date(s) or number of units of each fund that was sold. Gains or losses on sale must show the date the asset was sold, the full description of the asset (including number of shares or units), the appraised value, the sale price, and the resulting gain or loss. (3) If there were 1,669.046 units of T. Rowe Price and 669.365 units of Vanguard as of 7/31/24, what happened to the other units that were on hand the beginning of the accounting period? The accounting has no informational schedule. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] (4) The ending date of the accounting period is 7/31/24. But the Marriot[t] retirement plan holdings were sold on that date? (5) The disbursements schedule, even as supplemented (not "amended") does not set forth any dates covered by the payments to the residential care facility or adequate descriptions (i.e., date covered by each payment). Why was the payment on 2/5/24 so high? (6) Reimbursements to any individual must indicate who the original payee was. Whom did Jennifer Tang pay for medication? Deadline to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is by 6/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE COHOLAN FAMILY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 14, 1990 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement, Dismissing Action And Retaining Jurisdiction Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Note: Paragraph 2 of the proposed order requires petitioner to file a request for dismissal of her underlying petitions, but neither the prayer for relief included in the settlement petition nor the settlement agreement addressed how dismissal should be procured. Are the parties asking the Court to exercise its discretion to order dismissal without prejudice under CCP section 664.6(a) at this hearing or to merely take the underlying petitions off calendar without ordering dismissal and let petitioner file a request for dismissal after this hearing if she chooses to do so? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The correct form of notice for a petition filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 in mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) A copy of the operative trust agreement is not attached to the petition for the Court to be able to determine who is entitled to notice. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/4/25. A copy of any supplement or amendment to the petition must also be served after it is e-filed. [CRC 7.53(b).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES S. BUCKLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Many of the numbers of shares of securities sold are obscured on Schedules B and D. (2) Was the executor unable to learn from the bank what the two unidentified checks were? (3) Was the Wells Fargo advisory fee credit not a refund? And what does "redeemed cash" from the executor's client trust account mean? (4) The accounting shows assets having a FMV of $1,727.100.25 on hand as of 12/31/24. Proposed distribution must show the exact amount of cash, as of a date certain, and the complete description of each individual security, including Treasury bonds. [CRC 7.651(b).] (5) Attorney costs must be itemized, showing the date of each payment, the payee, the description, and the amount, not lump sums without any dates. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner, which made reviewing the petition and supporting papers challenging. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] For decedents' estates, only the ending balances of an accounting need to be verified by original statements.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA MOLLER LYONS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/4/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel on 4/25/25 with the following issues: 1) A declaration was filed on 4/16/25 which indicated that the balance of Wells Fargo 6480 as of 10/31/24 was $665,675.80; however: 1. There is no account listed on the Assets on Hand schedule attached to the Executor's declaration on 3/21/25 with that balance amount. 2. None of the other cash accounts listed in the Assets on Hand schedule have been verified. 3. The balance date must be for the end of business day 11/1/24, which is the end of account date, not 10/31/24. 4. A verified declaration pursuant to Local Rules 14.59A was not filed. 2) Order: a. Paragraph 7 of the order states that $177,825.30 is the reserve amount; however, that amount should remain $100,000 (as the difference in the extraordinary fees will be distributed, rather than held as additional reserve). b. The distribution to the remainder beneficiaries should only state the total; distinguishing the total into two separate amounts is unnecessary and confusing. c. The total to be distributed to the remainder beneficiaries appears incorrect. Please check the calculation (it appears the amount should be $1,288,140.01, not $1,284,972.93). d. The caption of the order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution".

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELICE TSAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/16/25 for failure to address all issues in Examiner's Notes: 1. Satisfaction and release must be filed by the 3 creditors who petitioner alleges have all been paid. 2. Please specify the assets on hand and the as of date (e.g. as of 1/1/25: total cash of $100,000, 100 shares of XYZ stock, 50 shares of ABS stock, etc). 3. Please calculate the cash to be distributed (e.g. $10,000 cash on hand, less $5,000 fees, less $1,000 costs = $4,000 cash on hand to distribute). 4. Please specify what each beneficiary will receive (John Doe: $100 cash, 5 shares of ABC stock, 5 share of XYZ stock, etc.) All documents to be filed by 6/2/25.

For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required for failure to address Examiner's Notes sent on 9/23/24 with the following issues: 1. Sienna Lee, Darren Chiu, Kaylie Chiu, and Elleni Chiu were given notice at 1534 19th Ave, San Francisco, but their addresses are listed as 534 19th Ave, San Francisco in the petition. 2. Trustee and trust beneficiaries were not listed / identified in Item 8. Note: notice will be required to those that have not already been noticed. 3. Item 3f indicates that the will is dated 10/15/09, but the will attached to the petition is dated 5/29/23. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
